prompt.launchInstruction: Read TASK.md and SPEC.md in this folder, then build the described module into the NocoBase env named expagents using the nb CLI (ALWAYS pass -e expagents). Work autonomously across multiple turns, do not stop to ask me; when done write REPORT.md.

samples.msg: Field status enum item planning requires color
Field status enum item planning color #1890ff is invalid. Allowed colors: red, magenta, volcano, orange, gold, lime, green, cyan, blue, geekblue, purple, default
flowSurfaces addBlock defaultFilter is only supported on direct table/list/gridCard/calendar/kanban blocks
flowSurfaces addBlock defaultFilter expects FilterGroup like {\"logic\":\"$and\",\"items\":[]}: Invalid filter: logic must be '$and' or '$or'
flowSurfaces addBlock settings invalid: chart query.resource is required
flowSurfaces layout row '0' requires sizes definition
flowSurfaces layout row '0' requires sizes definition
flowSurfaces layout row '0' requires sizes definition
flowSurfaces layout sizes length of row 'row1' does not match columns length

outcome.finalText:
The Content Calendar module is fully built on the `expagents` instance. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Data Model (3 collections):**
- `cc_campaigns` — 6 business fields + status select (4 options)
- `cc_contents` — 8 business fields + status/channel selects, cover attachment, body richText
- `cc_tags` — name field; m2m linked to cc_contents through `cc_contents_tags`
- M2O relation: cc_contents.campaign → cc_campaigns + reverse o2m cc_campaigns.contents

**5 Pages under "Content Calendar" group:**
1. **Campaigns** — Table with View/Edit + Filter/AddNew; View drawer has Details block + Contents sub-table
2. **Contents** — Table with campaign relation column, 3 updateRecord inline actions (Submit for Review / Schedule / Publish)
3. **Editorial Calendar** — Calendar block (cc_contents, titleField=title, colorField=status, startField/endField=publish_date)
4. **Content Kanban** — Kanban grouped by status, cards show title + channel
5. **Content Overview** — Bar chart (by channel) + Pie chart (by status) on row 1, Line chart (by month) on row 2

**Seed Data:** 4 campaigns covering all statuses, 10 contents covering all 5 channels × 5 statuses, 6 tags (2 per content), publish_dates spread across 2025-2026

**Workflow:** "Auto Fill Publish Date" — collection trigger on cc_contents update when status=published, sets publish_date=$system.now if empty. Enabled.

**REPORT.md** written to `/tmp/repro-exp/m03/REPORT.md` with coverage breakdown and notes on what was unclear.

transcript.text: Read TASK.md and SPEC.md in this folder, then build the described module into the NocoBase env named expagents using the nb CLI (ALWAYS pass -e expagents). Work autonomously across multiple turns, do not stop to ask me; when done write REPORT.md.
I'll read the task and spec files first.

transcript.cmd: {"file_path": "/tmp/repro-exp/m03/TASK.md"}
{"file_path": "/tmp/repro-exp/m03/SPEC.md"}

transcript.text: I'll start by exploring the available CLI capabilities, then build the module systematically.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "nb api data-modeling --help -e expagents 2>&1 | head -80", "description": "Check data-modeling CLI help"}
{"command": "nb api flow-surfaces --help -e expagents 2>&1 | head -80", "description": "Check flow-surfaces CLI help"}
{"command": "nb api data-modeling fields --help -e expagents 2>&1 | head -60", "description": "Check fields help"}
{"command": "nb api flow-surfaces --help -e expagents 2>&1 | tail -60", "description": "Check remaining flow-surfaces commands"}
{"command": "nb api data-modeling collections --help -e expagents 2>&1", "description": "Check collections help"}
{"command": "nb api data-modeling collections apply --help -e expagents 2>&1", "description": "Check collections apply help"}
{"command": "nb api data-modeling fields apply --help -e expagents 2>&1", "description": "Check fields apply help"}

transcript.text: Now I'll start building. First, creating the `cc_campaigns` collection.

transcript.cmd:
{"command": "nb api data-modeling collections apply \
  --name cc_campaigns \
  --title "Campaigns" \
  --template general \
  --fields '[
    {"name":"name","title":"Name","interface":"input"},
    {"name":"objective","title":"Objective","interface":"input"},
    {"name":"start_date","title":"Start Date","interface":"date"},
    {"name":"end_date","title":"End Date","interface":"date"},
    {"name":"status","title":"Status","interface":"select","enum":[{"label":"Planning","value":"planning"},{"label":"Acti

  --name cc_campaigns \
  --title "Campaigns" \
  --template general \
  --fields '[
    {"name":"name","title":"Name","interface":"input"},
    {"name":"objective","title":"Objective","interface":"input"},
    {"name":"start_date","title":"Start Date","interface":"date"},
    {"name":"end_date","title":"End Date","interface":"date"},
    {"name":"status","title":"Status","interface":"select","enum":[{"label":"Planning","value":"planning","color":"#1890f

{"command": "nb api data-modeling collections apply \
  --name cc_campaigns \
  --title "Campaigns" \
  --template general \
  --fields '[
    {"name":"name","title":"Name","interface":"input"},
    {"name":"objective","title":"Objective","interface":"input"},
    {"name":"start_date","title":"Start Date","interface":"date"},
    {"name":"end_date","title":"End Date","interface":"date"},
    {"name":"status","title":"Status","interface":"select","enum":[{"label":"Planning","value":"planning","color":"blue"

transcript.text: `cc_campaigns` created. Now creating `cc_contents` with the m2o relationship.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "nb api data-modeling collections apply \\\n  --name cc_contents \\\n  --title \"Contents\" \\\n  --template general \\\n  --fields '[\n    {\"name\":\"title\",\"title\":\"Title\",\"interface\":\"input\"},\n    {\"name\":\"channel\",\"title\":\"Channel\",\"interface\":\"select\",\"enum\":[{\"label\":\"Blog\",\"value\":\"blog\",\"color\":\"blue\"},{\"label\":\"Email\",\"value\":\"email\",\"color\":\"cyan\"},{\"label\":\"Social\",\"value\":\"social\",\"color\":\"green\"},{\"label\":\"Video\",\"value\":\"video\",\"color\":\"red\"},{\"label\":\"Webinar\",\"value\":\"webinar\",\"color\"

transcript.text: Now creating the m2o relationship from `cc_contents` to `cc_campaigns` with the reverse hasMany.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "nb api data-modeling fields apply \\\n  --collection-name cc_contents \\\n  --name campaign \\\n  --title \"Campaign\" \\\n  --interface \"m2o\" \\\n  --target cc_campaigns \\\n  --reverse-name contents \\\n  --reverse-title \"Contents\" \\\n  --reverse-interface \"o2m\" \\\n  -e expagents 2>&1", "description": "Create m2o relation from cc_contents to cc_campaigns with reverse o2m"}

transcript.text: Good. Let me verify the fields are set up correctly on both collections.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "nb api data-modeling collections fields list --collection-name cc_campaigns -e expagents -j 2>&1 | grep -E '\"name\"|\"type\"|\"title\"' | head -50", "description": "List cc_campaigns fields"}

{"command": "nb api data-modeling collections fields list --collection-name cc_contents -e expagents -j 2>&1 | grep -E '\"name\"|\"type\"|\"title\"' | head -60", "description": "List cc_contents fields"}

transcript.text: Both collections are set up correctly. Now let me explore the flow-surfaces page creation commands.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "nb api flow-surfaces add-block --help -e expagents 2>&1 | head -100", "description": "Check add-block help"}
{"command": "nb api flow-surfaces catalog --help -e expagents 2>&1 | head -40", "description": "Check catalog help"}
{"command": "nb api flow-surfaces get --help -e expagents 2>&1 | head -40", "description": "Check get command help"}
{"command": "nb api flow-surfaces --help -e expagents 2>&1 | grep -E \"^  api flow\" | head -30", "description": "List all flow-surfaces commands"}
{"command": "nb api flow-surfaces create-menu --help -e expagents 2>&1", "description": "Check create-menu help"}
{"command": "nb api flow-surfaces create-page --help -e expagents 2>&1", "description": "Check create-page help"}

transcript.text: Now I'll create the menu group and pages. Starting with the "Content Calendar" menu group.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "nb api flow-surfaces create-menu \\\n  --title \"Content Calendar\" \\\n  --type group \\\n  --icon \"CalendarOutlined\" \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Create Content Calendar menu group"}

{"command": "# Create \"Campaigns\" menu item under the group\nnb api flow-surfaces create-menu \\\n  --title \"Campaigns\" \\\n  --type item \\\n  --icon \"ProjectOutlined\" \\\n  --parent-menu-route-id \"368080742055936\" \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Create Campaigns menu item"}

{"command": "# Create \"Contents\" menu item under the group\nnb api flow-surfaces create-menu \\\n  --title \"Contents\" \\\n  --type item \\\n  --icon \"FileTextOutlined\" \\\n  --parent-menu-route-id \"368080742055936\" \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Create Contents menu item"}

transcript.text: Menu items created. Let me check the page surface to understand the current state.

transcript.text: The pages are initialized. Now I'll add the Table block to the Campaigns page.

# Create campaign 2: Q3 Webinar Series / planning

  --values '{"name":"Q3 Webinar Series","objective":"Educate prospects and nurture pipeline throu

  --values "{\"title\":\"5 Ways Spring Changes How We Work\",\"channel\":\"blog\",\"status\":\"idea\",\"publish_date\":\"2026-04-10\",\"author\":\"Diana Reyes\",\"brief\":\"A thought leadership piece exploring s

transcript.text: Seed data added. Now starting on the enhanced features. Adding `cover` and `body` fields to `cc_contents`.

transcript.cmd:
{"command": "# Add cover (attachment) field
nb api data-modeling fields apply \
  --collection-name cc_contents \
  --name cover \
  --title "Cover" \
  --interface "attachment" \
  -e expagents -j 2>&1 | grep -E '"name"|"type"|"error"'

# Add body (vditor richtext) field
nb api data-modeling fields apply \
  --collection-name cc_contents \
  --name body \
  --title "Body" \
  --interface "richText" \
  -e expagents -j 2>&1 | grep -E '"name"|"type"|"error"'", "description": "Add cover and body fields to cc_contents"}

{"command": "# Create cc_tags collection\nnb api data-modeling collections apply \\\n  --name cc_tags \\\n  --title \"Tags\" \\\n  --template general \\\n  --fields '[{\"name\":\"name\",\"title\":\"Name\",\"interface\":\"input\"}]' \\\n  --settings '{\"titleField\":\"name\"}' \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1 | grep -E '\"name\"|\"title\"|\"template\"' | head -5", "description": "Create cc_tags collection"}

{"command": "# Create m2m relation: cc_contents.tags \u2194 cc_tags.contents\nnb api data-modeling fields apply \\\n  --collection-name cc_contents \\\n  --name tags \\\n  --title \"Tags\" \\\n  --interface \"m2m\" \\\n  --target cc_tags \\\n  --reverse-name contents \\\n  --reverse-title \"Contents\" \\\n  --reverse-interface \"m2m\" \\\n  --settings '{\"through\":\"cc_contents_tags\",\"foreignKey\":\"ccContentId\",\"otherKey\":\"ccTagId\"}' \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1 | grep -E '\"name\"|\"type\"|\"error\"' | head -5", "description": "Create m2m relation between cc_contents and cc_tags"}

transcript.text: Now creating 6 seed tags.

transcript.cmd:
{"command": "# Create 6 tags
for TAG in "Product Launch" "SEO" "Brand Story" "Customer Success" "Thought Leadership" "Event Promo"; do
  nb api resource create --resource cc_tags \
    --values "{\"name\":\"$TAG\"}" \
    -e expagents -j 2>&1 | grep '"id"' | head -1
done", "description": "Create 6 seed tags"}

transcript.text: Tags associated. Now creating enhanced pages. Starting with the Editorial Calendar page.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "# Create \"Editorial Calendar\" menu item\nnb api flow-surfaces create-menu \\\n  --title \"Editorial Calendar\" \\\n  --type item \\\n  --icon \"ScheduleOutlined\" \\\n  --parent-menu-route-id \"368080742055936\" \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Create Editorial Calendar menu item"}

{"command": "# Add Calendar block to Editorial Calendar page\nnb api flow-surfaces add-block \\\n  --target '{\"uid\":\"y1j2wdayerw\"}' \\\n  --type \"calendar\" \\\n  --resource '{\"collectionName\":\"cc_contents\",\"dataSourceKey\":\"main\"}' \\\n  --settings '{\"titleField\":\"title\",\"startField\":\"publish_date\",\"endField\":\"publish_date\",\"colorField\":\"status\"}' \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Add Calendar block to Editorial Calendar page"}

transcript.text: Now creating the Content Kanban page.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "# Create \"Content Kanban\" menu item\nnb api flow-surfaces create-menu \\\n  --title \"Content Kanban\" \\\n  --type item \\\n  --icon \"AppstoreOutlined\" \\\n  --parent-menu-route-id \"368080742055936\" \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Create Content Kanban menu item"}

{"command": "# Add Kanban block to Content Kanban page\nnb api flow-surfaces add-block \\\n  --target '{\"uid\":\"rl7vgg23aqa\"}' \\\n  --type \"kanban\" \\\n  --resource '{\"collectionName\":\"cc_contents\",\"dataSourceKey\":\"main\"}' \\\n  --settings '{\"groupField\":\"status\"}' \\\n  --fields '[\"title\",\"channel\"]' \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Add Kanban block to Content Kanban page"}

transcript.text: Now creating the Content Overview dashboard page with charts.

transcript.cmd: {"command": "# Create \"Content Overview\" menu item\nnb api flow-surfaces create-menu \\\n  --title \"Content Overview\" \\\n  --type item \\\n  --icon \"BarChartOutlined\" \\\n  --parent-menu-route-id \"368080742055936\" \\\n  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Create Content Overview menu item"}

{"command": "# Add "Submit for review" updateRecord action
nb api flow-surfaces add-record-action \
  --target '{"uid":"xi98zon7cd0"}' \
  --type "updateRecord" \
  --settings '{"title":"Submit for Review","assignValues":{"status":"in_review"}}' \
  -e expagents -j 2>&1

# Add "Schedule" updateRecord action
nb api flow-surfaces add-record-action \
  --target '{"uid":"xi98zon7cd0"}' \
  --type "updateRecord" \

nb api workflow workflows create \

  --type "collection" \
  --config '{
    "collectionName":"cc_contents",
    "mode":2,
    "condition":{"status":"published"},
    "appends":[]
  }' \
  -e expagents -j 2>&1", "description": "Create collection trigger workflow for auto-filling publish date"}

transcript.cmd:
{"file_path": "/tmp/repro-exp/m03/REPORT.md", "content": "# Build Report: Content Calendar Module (expagents)

## 1. What Was Built

### Collections / Data Model

| Collection | Template | Key Fields | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `cc_campaigns` | general | name (title), objective, start_date (date), end_date (date), status (select: planning/active/completed/archived), owner | titleField = name |
| `cc_contents` | general | title (title), channel (select: blog/email/social/video/webinar), status (select: idea/draft/in_review/scheduled/published), publish_date (date), author, brief (tex

transcript.text:
The Content Calendar module is fully built on the `expagents` instance. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Data Model (3 collections):**
- `cc_campaigns` — 6 business fields + status select (4 options)
- `cc_contents` — 8 business fields + status/channel selects, cover attachment, body richText
- `cc_tags` — name field; m2m linked to cc_contents through `cc_contents_tags`
- M2O relation: cc_contents.campaign → cc_campaigns + reverse o2m cc_campaigns.contents

**5 Pages under "Content Calendar" group:**
1. **Campaigns** — Table with View/Edit + Filter/AddNew; View drawer has Details block + Contents sub-table
2. **Contents** — Table with campaign relation column, 3 updateRecord inline actions (Submit for Review / Schedule / Publish)
3. **Editorial Calendar** — Calendar block (cc_contents, titleField=title, colorField=status, startField/endField=publish_date)
4. **Content Kanban** — Kanban grouped by status, cards show title + channel
5. **Content Overview** — Bar chart (by channel) + Pie chart (by status) on row 1, Line chart (by month) on row 2

**Seed Data:** 4 campaigns covering all statuses, 10 contents covering all 5 channels × 5 statuses, 6 tags (2 per content), publish_dates spread across 2025-2026

**Workflow:** "Auto Fill Publish Date" — collection trigger on cc_contents update when status=published, sets publish_date=$system.now if empty. Enabled.

**REPORT.md** written to `/tmp/repro-exp/m03/REPORT.md` with coverage breakdown and notes on what was unclear.
